description: Letter from ʿImrān b. Yaḥyā Ibn al-Rofe, unknown location, to Eliyyahu the Judge, in Fustat. In Judaeo-Arabic. The addressee opens with the death of his son Yaḥyā. He apparently moves on to business matters, since he mentions "cloves" three lines further down. Recto was reused for pen trials of ornate Hebrew letters, and verso was reused for what looks almost like an architectural drawing. (Information in part from Goitein's index cards and CUDL.)
description_he: מכתב מעמראן ב' יחיא אבן הרופא לאליהו השופט, בפוסטאט. ביהודית-ערבית. הנמען פותח במות בנו יחיא ועובר לענייני עסקים, מזכיר ציפורן.
